title: Artificial Intelligence Policy Advisor
description: An Artificial Intelligence Policy Advisor helps governments and organizations develop and implement policies related to the ethical and responsible use of AI. They analyze the societal, economic, and legal implications of AI technologies and advise on regulations, guidelines, and best practices.
content: AI Policy Advisors work at the intersection of technology, law, and ethics. They need a strong understanding of AI technologies, policy-making processes, and the Indian regulatory landscape. This career involves research, analysis, stakeholder engagement, and communication to shape the future of AI in India.

financialFeasibility.note: While postgraduate education is essential, scholarships and educational loans are available, making this career accessible to motivated students from middle-class backgrounds. Internships can provide valuable experience and financial support.

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in government agencies (e.g., NITI Aayog, MeitY), think tanks, consulting firms, and large tech companies. The private sector is increasingly investing in AI ethics and governance.

relatedCareers.reason: Conducts research and analysis to inform policy decisions.
relatedCareers.title: Policy Analyst

relatedCareers.reason: Applies statistical and machine learning techniques to analyze data and extract insights.
relatedCareers.title: Data Scientist

relatedCareers.reason: Provides legal expertise on AI-related issues, such as data privacy and intellectual property.
relatedCareers.title: Lawyer

genderDistribution.note: The field is relatively new, offering opportunities for both men and women. Efforts are being made to promote gender diversity in AI and related fields.
